title: Lesson 44 — Passion and Apathy

words.definition: showing strong, forceful, or passionate feeling
words.example: She issued a vehement denial within the hour.

words.definition: showing little interest, enthusiasm, or concern
words.example: Turnout was low among apathetic young voters.

words.definition: having no particular interest or concern; neutral
words.example: He seemed indifferent to which restaurant they chose.

words.definition: feeling or appearing casually calm and unconcerned
words.example: He gave a nonchalant shrug and changed the subject.

words.definition: unimpressed or indifferent, especially through overfamiliarity
words.example: Frequent flyers grow blasé about even the best views.

words.definition: showing a casual lack of concern; carefree
words.example: Her insouciant attitude toward deadlines worried her boss.

words.definition: enduring pain or hardship without showing feelings or complaint
words.example: He remained stoic through the entire painful procedure.
An ardent supporter who never missed a rally.

words.definition: having or showing great enthusiasm for an activity or interest
words.example: An avid reader who finished a book a week.

words.definition: a person who is fanatical and uncompromising in pursuit of their beliefs
words.example: A zealot on the committee blocked every compromise.
A fervent plea for calm went unheeded.

words.definition: unable to be affected or influenced by something
words.example: He seemed impervious to criticism of any kind.

story: The two roommates watched the news very differently. Marco reacted with [[vehement|vehement]] outrage to every headline; Priya stayed almost [[apathetic|apathetic]], genuinely [[indifferent|indifferent]] to most of it, offering only a [[nonchalant|nonchalant]] shrug. Some called her [[blase|blasé]], even [[insouciant|insouciant]], but she preferred to think of herself as [[stoic|stoic]]. Marco was the opposite: [[ardent|ardent]] about every cause, an [[avid|avid]] petition-signer some friends jokingly called a [[zealot|zealot]]. His [[fervent|fervent]] arguments never once moved her — she remained [[impervious|impervious]] to his passion, just as he stayed baffled by her calm.
